scope: src/lib/courseAdapter.ts, src/app/hooks/useLessonPlayerState.ts, plus test files and mock updates
intent: Fix companion PDFs that appear in sidebar but fail to render in lesson player by adding a getLesson() point-lookup to the adapter interface and updating useLessonPlayerState to use it instead of filtering getLessons(). Also add lessonResolved state to properly dismiss skeleton when lesson metadata resolution completes.
reviewers: adversarial (~45 lines executable code, state transitions, async flows)

pre_existing.title: Null lesson metadata renders default video UI, not error state
pre_existing.why_it_matters: When getLesson() returns null for a genuinely missing lesson ID, the hook sets lessonType=null and lessonResolved=true. The UnifiedLessonPlayer error state (line 321) only triggers on `error || !adapter`, not on resolved-but-null lesson metadata. The user sees default video player UI instead of 'Lesson not found'. The old code showed an infinite skeleton (worse); the new code dismisses the skeleton but still shows inappropriate content.
pre_existing.suggested_fix: Add a condition in UnifiedLessonPlayer to show the 'Lesson not found' error state when lessonTypeResolved is true and lessonType is null.
evidence: UnifiedLessonPlayer.tsx:321 — error state gated on `error || !adapter`, no check for null lessonType with resolved state
useLessonPlayerState.ts:152 — sets lessonType to match?.type ?? null, which is null when getLesson returns null
useLessonPlayerState.ts:158 — sets lessonResolved to true in success path even when match is null
LessonContentRenderer.tsx:74 — only checks !lessonTypeResolved for skeleton; no error fallback for null lessonType

requirements_completeness.R1: met — getLesson finds companion PDFs, lessonType='pdf', PdfContent renders
requirements_completeness.R2: met — getLesson searches all PDFs, getLessons unchanged
requirements_completeness.R4: met — getLesson searches videos first, same metadata extraction
requirements_completeness.Unit1: met — interface updated, both adapters implement getLesson
requirements_completeness.Unit2: met — useLessonPlayerState uses getLesson point-lookup, lessonResolved added

residual_risks: YouTubeCourseAdapter.getLesson() duplicates LessonItem construction from getLessons() — two code paths building the same object shape could diverge over time (pre-existing pattern, not introduced by this diff)
When getLesson() returns null, LessonContentRenderer falls through to video player rendering — better than infinite skeleton but still not a proper 'not found' UX
testing_gaps: No end-to-end integration test verifying sidebar click → lesson player → PDF renders (plan lists this as manual verification)
No test for the case where getLesson() returns null and the UnifiedLessonPlayer renders (pre-existing gap)
learnings: Related solution: docs/solutions/ui-bugs/course-content-sidebar-pdf-discoverability-2026-05-03.md — the sidebar fix that exposed this resolution gap
Related solution: docs/solutions/integration-issues/lesson-chrome-store-consumer-integration-gaps-2026-05-02.md — precedent for store-consumer wiring gaps
